The '''copula''' in [[Aelaki]] is expressed through three distinct constructions depending on the semantic type of predication.

== Identity / Class Membership ==

Identity and class membership ("X is a Y") use the '''irregular verb ''zon-(ki)'''''. The [[Ki_syllables|-(ki)]] suffix indicates what gender the group should be referred to by.

When listing multiple class memberships, '''zen-(ki)''' is used to close the list.

=== Example ===

: ''Corbin Bleu '''Zonash''' Actor '''Zenash''''' = "Corbin Bleu is an actor"

Here, ''zonash'' introduces the class ("actor") and ''zenash'' closes the predication. The '''-ash''' [[Ki_syllables|Ki suffix]] marks third person singular [[Male|male]] agreement.

== Predicative ==

Predicative copular sentences ("Apples are tasty") are expressed using '''stative verbs'''. There is no overt copula; the stative verb itself carries the predication.

: "Apples are tasty" → expressed with the stative verb for "tasty"

== Existential ==

Existential sentences ("Apples exist", "There are apples") use the '''stative verb ''Lake'''''.

: "Apples exist" → ''Lake'' + appropriate [[Ki_syllables|Ki]] agreement

== Historical Notes ==

The original copula '''ghi''' derives from a word meaning "hold". The irregular second person copula '''gi''' eventually shifted in function: as word order became freer, ''gi'' was reanalyzed as a '''yes-no question particle''', losing its original copular meaning.

[[Category:Aelaki grammar]]
